summary.intent: Fix month-long Nightly CI breakage caused by surviving mutation testing (missed mutants) across ars-core, ars-collections, and ars-components, without running mutation testing locally. Address all currently applicable found mutants and push a PR, with a follow-up planned for the next Nightly run.
summary.outcome: Added focused test coverage across 10 source files targeting 261 missed mutants from the June 16 Nightly artifacts. All local gates passed (focused tests, xfmt, xclippy, xci --profile fast). Branch committed and pushed; PR created targeting main. No production behavior changes and no mutants.toml excludes were required.

repo: Nightly mutation artifacts are retained for only 14 days, not a full month — historical May reports beyond that window require job log ##[warning] scraping as the only durable breadcrumb
Mutation matrix runs in named shards (core-shard-1, core-shard-2, collections-shard-3, components-shard-N); component shards are frequently cancelled at the 6-hour job cap
Required repo gates in order: cargo xfmt, cargo xclippy, cargo xci --profile fast (covers fmt, clippy, unit/nextest 8711 tests, ars-i18n stages, adapter, spec-conformance, coverage-native)
Commits require Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com> trailer and must be followed by the waiting-for-codex-review loop
No .cargo/mutants.toml exists — adding equivalent-mutant excludes would introduce a new policy surface and was avoided in favor of direct behavior tests
file_upload tests live in a sibling tests.rs file rather than an inline #[cfg(test)] module
scroll_area scroll_progress missed mutants were already addressed by code on HEAD that was one commit ahead of the failing Nightly SHA

code.finding: Freed size from panel collapse is distributed to the right-side panel first, then left — tests that assumed left-first distribution were wrong and had to be corrected

Existing image_cropper tests only asserted event shape and broad invariants, not exact arithmetic deltas — this left resize-handle geometry, rotation normalization, and shortcut key values all unconstrained by tests
New locale-format tests produced unused_qualifications warnings that required cleanup before clippy gate passed

Color boundary math, HSB/HSL/RGB conversions, WCAG thresholds, perceptual naming bucket edges, channel replacement, and format alpha branching were all unconstrained — mutations in these areas survived because tests only checked broad invariants
Typeahead timeout boundary, locale wrapper delegation, disabled filtering, and wrap/non-wrap index arithmetic had test gaps that allowed surviving mutants

workflow: Downloading all mutation artifacts at once with --pattern 'mutants-*' is unreliable and slow; download specific shard names individually into separate directories to avoid filename collisions
Multiple gh run download calls targeting the same directory collide when archives share root-level filenames (e.g., missed.txt) — always use per-artifact directories
cargo test -p <crate> <filter1> <filter2> with multiple space-separated filters does not work as expected; run one filter per invocation

Broad month-wide artifact sweeps and log scrapes were impractically slow; latest retained missed set is sufficient as the actionable target, with older expired data checked only for shard pass/fail status
Job log ##[warning] lines are the durable missed-mutant record after artifact expiry — gh run view <id> --log-failed | rg '##\[warning\]' is the fallback
Test-first approach (adding assertions that pin exact behavior mutants change) was uniformly more appropriate than adding mutants.toml excludes for this codebase

friction: gh run download --pattern 'mutants-*' timed out on the full shard set; required switching to targeted per-artifact downloads
Multiple artifacts sharing the same root-level filename (missed.txt) caused silent file collisions when downloaded to the same directory
Month-wide log scrape across all failed runs and broad all_missed.tsv generation both ran too long to complete and had to be cancelled
Sandbox network restrictions initially blocked gh run list, requiring a retry after network access was granted
cargo xclippy required fetching wasm-bindgen-test-macro over the network, blocking on first attempt
Initial splitter collapse test asserted left-side distribution priority when the implementation actually distributes to the right side first
cargo xci --profile fast required many long polling waits across compilation, nextest (8711 tests), i18n stages, adapter, and coverage-native phases
open_items: Follow-up PR planned for after the next Nightly run to address any remaining or newly surfaced missed mutants
ars-components shards were cancelled at the 6-hour CI cap — their missed mutant reports were partial; some component mutants may still survive in the next Nightly run
Expired May artifacts could not be fully verified; only current shard pass/fail status was used to determine applicability of older mutants

No equivalent-mutant excludes were added — if any of the retained 261 mutants are genuinely unobservable, they will reappear in the next Nightly and require a mutants.toml exclude entry with documented rationale
